claims.claim: EMC has a higher maximum explosion pressure (0.62 MPa) and rate of pressure rise (35.90 MPa/s) compared to DMC (0.59 MPa and 27.50 MPa/s) and BVG (0.45 MPa and 25.20 MPa/s), measured at 393 K and 0.10 MPa initial conditions in an 8 L cylindrical vessel.
claims.quote: The PMax and (dp/dt)Max of EMC are higher than those of DMC and BVG. For EMC, they are are 0.62 MPa and 35.8 MPa/s, respectively. For DMC, they are 0.59 MPa and 27.50 MPa/s, respectively. For BVG, they are 0.45 MPa and 25.20 MPa/s, respectively.

claims.claim: The flammability limits for EMC, DMC, and BVG were determined to be 2.35%–14.65%, 3.50%–17.00%, and 10.20%–47.50%, respectively, at 393 K.
claims.quote: The flammability limits for EMC, DMC, and BVG were determined to be 2.35 % to 14.65 %, 3.50 % to 17.00 %, and 10.20 % to 47.50 %, respectively.

claims.claim: As carbonate mixture content increases in mixtures with BVG, the LFL decreases while maximum overpressure, maximum rate of pressure rise, and LOC all increase.
claims.quote: The addition of carbonate mixtures to BVG results in a decrease in the LFL value, while PMax, (dp/dt)Max and LOC values all increase.

claims.claim: As temperature rises from 298 K to 393 K, the LOC of BVG decreases from 7.39% to 7.03%.
claims.quote: The results indicate that the LOC of BVG gradually decreases with increasing temperature. As the temperature rises from 298 K to 393 K, the LOC of BVG decreases from 7.39 % to 7.03 %.

claims.claim: When carbonate mixture content increases in BVG mixtures, PMax increases from 0.45 MPa to 0.61 MPa, (dp/dt)Max increases from 25.20 MPa/s to 35.50 MPa/s, and KG increases from 5.00 to 7.10 (MPa·m)/s, with LOC increasing from 7.03% to 10.80%.
claims.quote: As carbonate mixture contents increases, the PMax of BVG increases from 0.45 MPa to 0.61 MPa, and (dp/dt)Max increases from 25.20 MPa/s to 35.50 MPa/s, and KG increases from 5.00 (MPa⋅m)/s to 7.10 (MPa⋅m)/s... the LOC of mixtures of carbonates and BVG increased from 7.03 % to 10.80 %.
